tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine is cloudy, brown, with ammoniacal odor, and contains ketones and bilirubin, but no nitrites or leukocytes, making infection less likely. HbA1c is low, excluding chronic hyperglycemia. Findings are consistent with possible acute or chronic kidney disease; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
